insecure_http.title: Keenetic API is using plaintext HTTP
insecure_http.description:
The Keenetic Router Pro integration ({title}) is connecting to {host} over plain HTTP. Your router username, password (NDW2 hash), and session cookie traverse the LAN unencrypted on every poll, so anyone on the same network — including untrusted Wi-Fi guests, IoT devices, or a compromised LAN host — can capture credentials and impersonate you to the router.

Mitigation:
1. In the router web UI, enable HTTPS for the web admin (System → Components → ensure SSL/TLS support is installed; Management → Web Admin → enable HTTPS).
2. Open the integration entry in Settings → Devices & services → Keenetic Router Pro → Reconfigure, switch SSL on, and update the port (typically 443).
3. After confirming HTTPS works, rotate the router admin password — anyone who sniffed the LAN since the integration was set up may already have it.

This warning is suppressed for loopback addresses.
